toc.text: The Voice Toys system is built with the desire for users to love it
VoiceToys are not toys, even though they are called that.
What are the elements of the system and what are they for?

chunks: Thank you for your trust!
SpreadY device in operation
It is intended for work in speech therapy and defectology practice, but it can be used in work
with users of all ages and levels of development.
It was developed with love and the desire to fit into the work process so that it does not interfere with it, but
to motivate, accelerate progress and facilitate the therapist's work.
It is designed to be easy to use, visually reduced, with simple shapes and clear,
vivid colors, unusual, but natural and humane. It is made of harmless, high-quality materials
material.
It is controlled wirelessly, with an original app developed for Android and iOS devices.
It is based on microcontrollers that are very easy to update the operating system wirelessly
system. Thanks to this, devices can be modified and enriched with additional ones
functions according to user requirements. We invite you to participate with your ideas
in the development and progress of this unique system!
The Voice Toys system is built with the desire for users to love it
1 - General information
safety first!
VoiceToys are not toys, even though they are called that.
Do not give VoiceToys devices to children on their own
using!
All VoiceToys devices are made of harmless materials
material and without sharp edges to minimize
risk of injury. The devices are intended for them
handled by therapists. Apart from the device VibeY, which produces
vibrations and is intended to be touched by the user, others
do not give the devices to the users in order to
avoid accidental damage. Pay special attention to
speakers from the SpaceY system, because they are emitting
surfaces made of soft wood. Catch them too
hold only the plastic part!
Note on user safety and responsibility
This manual is intended to help you in the correct i
the safe use of this electronic device. Before
of the device, read this manual carefully and
follow all the guidelines listed. The manufacturer does not
bears responsibility for improper use of the device or
the consequences that may arise from it.
IMPORTANT!!! Do not attempt to repair the devices yourself,
nor to open or unfold them for any reason!
In case of any problem with any part
system, failure or breakage, contact the manufacturer directly or
to your local representative from whom you purchased the device.
VoiceToys wireless devices contain rechargeable batteries
lithium-ion batteries, which in case of wrong
handling can cause fire! Do not expose them to high
temperatures or direct sunlight! Uređaji ne
may come into contact with water!
Disposal and recycling
The housings of the devices are made of PET-G plastic,
aluminum and clirite. They also contain various electronic ones
components and lithium-ion batteries. In the case of the end
life cycle, do not throw them in the municipal waste, but
contact your local recycling center or directly
device manufacturer.
system overview
The VoiceToys system consists of four
device groups and mobile apps:
SpreadY makes 5 multipurpose
wirelessly connected to each other
pillar. They are primarily intended
visual representation of the expanse
voice in space, but through mobile
the application can be activated by others
functionality (memory game,
analysis and synthesis of words and sentences, etc.)
JumpY is a light panel that
shows or reacts to the sound level
changing the distance of the object from
wireless sensor that comes with
device. It is intended for visual
volume representation and
sensorimotor stimulation and exercise,
using original games that
activated via a mobile application.
VibeY is a vibrotactile device that
reacts to the intensity and pitch of the sound
producing soft rainbow lights
color and gentle vibrations. It is intended for
voice stimulation and height control i
voice intensity. Sensitivity can
control with a mobile application.
SpaceY are 5 smart speakers that
serve for identification and localization
sound in space. They control themselves
exclusively by mobile application.
Devices from the VoiceToys system
What are the elements of the system and what are they for?
intended?
CONTENTS AND SAFETY NOTICES
What's in the box?
The VoiceToys system comes in two boxes. They are packed in one
wireless devices from the system, as separate devices, each in its own
in a separate box, marked by colors:
• 1 piece - VibeY
• 5 pieces - SpaceY (blue, yellow, green, red, gray)
• 5 pieces - SpreadY (blue, yellow, green, red, gray)
They also come with additional components for work:
• 5 pieces of transparent bars for SpreadY
• 5 card holders for SpreadY
• 1 charging cable
In the second box comes the device JumpY, with the associated
power supply and distance sensor - JumpY Sensor, which
also treated as a wireless device..
Unpacking and setting up for work
Take the wireless devices out of the box and place them horizontally
position. Put the boxes in a safe place, just in case
future transport or disposal. If you think that you
are not required, you can return them to the manufacturer for recycling.
The JumpY device is placed on the wall, according to the instructions in
chapter dedicated to detailed information about it.
Device power supply and electrical safety
All devices are powered by standard USB chargers of 5V/
3A using a cable with a USB-C connector. Do not use
faulty or damaged cables, but only those that you have
supplied with the device or adequate quality replacements. During
charging DO NOT LEAVE DEVICES UNATTENDED! U
in the event of an unusually high temperature
device, the appearance of smoke or unusual odors device
Turn off the power IMMEDIATELY and put it away
may cause fire! Wireless devices charge and are intended
for operation without a connected power supply, while the JumpY device must
to be constantly connected to the power supply because it does not contain
batteries.
Explanation of symbols:
- the device is certified for safety according to
standards for the market of the Republic of Serbia
- the device is certified for safety according to
standards for the European Union market
- do not throw the device in the communal waste, but
recycle or send to manufacturer.
WARNINGS
Sign
Meaning
⚠️ Warning
Indicates the possibility of serious physical injury if the instructions in this manual are not followed properly.
▲ Caution
Indicates the possibility of physical injury or property damage if instructions are not followed properly.
🚫 Forbidden
A crossed out circle indicates that something is prohibited. An explanation of the prohibition is located next to the symbol.
Dismantling is not allowed!
CAUTION: The battery in the device is not replaceable and its replacement may cause risk. Do not attempt to replace or disconnect it.
CAUTION: Products are not intended for use in flammable or explosive environments.
WARNING: There is a risk of explosion or injury if the device is exposed to conductive materials, liquid, fire or high temperature (above 40°C).
WARNING: Do not use the device in a wet environment. Protect it from liquid penetration.
WARNING: The device must be powered by an external power supply that complies with the IEC/EN 62368-1 standard and meets ES1 and PS1 requirements.
WARNING: Risk of electric shock or fire. Charge the device exclusively with the supplied USB charger. The USB cable must be connected to a certified laptop/PC or a certified mini USB charger.
WARNING: DO NOT open the device case. If the case is found to be open, the warranty becomes void.

The mobile application "VoiceToys" is intended
for control, memory of parameters and
device update.

where you need to grant access
requested phone functions. After
approval, the application is installed on
common way and can be found among
other applications on your device, sub
with the name VoiceToys and with our logo.
During use it is necessary to
bluetooth communication and location on
your phone are turned on.

2 - 1: Device description and safety notes
VibeY is a vibrotactile, light-up box, intended for stimulation
voting and controlling the pitch and intensity of the voice. Although it is intended
to feel the vibrations with it, it must not be given to children
self handling! Detailed device settings are being made
via the VoiceToys mobile app.
2 - 2: Commissioning the device
The device is started by turning on the switch located with
bottom side of the device to position 1. After switching on, the logo
on the case starts to shine, the LED light on the top side shows
animation with lights and the device vibrates for a short time. This effect is
can be turned off via the mobile app, which is explained in
next chapter. Your VibeY device is now ready to go.
If the logo changes color to yellow, it means that it is a battery
weakened, and if it starts flashing red, the battery is at a critical level
low and should be recharged immediately. Battery status you can
can also be controlled using a mobile application.
There are also three ports on the bottom of the device
sound inside the device. Do not cover or place them
device on a soft surface that can absorb sound!
introductory remarks
VibeY , the underside of the device
2 - 3: Charging the device's battery
The device contains a rechargeable Lithium-ion battery
with a voltage of 5V/3A, using a USB-C connector with
the underside of the device. After connecting the voltage,
logo indicates charging by emitting red, yellow or
green color, according to the state of the battery. When it lights up
solid green, the charging process is complete.

3 -1: System description and safety notes
SpreadY is a multifunctional system consisting of 5
luminous pillars. They are intended for visual identification
sound propagation, as a memory game or learning
addition and for analysis and synthesis of words and sentences by inclusion
individual pillars. Selection of functions and detailed settings
devices are done through the VoiceToys mobile application.
3-2: Commissioning the system
SpreadY posts are used by spreading them in space
so that the blue pillar is closest to the sound source. The others
the pillars are arranged in the following order: green, yellow,
red, gray. The steps are put into operation by turning on the switch
from the bottom of the device to position 1.
After switching on, the logo on
the case starts to glow. Get ready
connect automatically, without any
additional setting and react to
sound immediately after switching on. Blue
the column is the main one, it has a microphone,
he sends a signal to the others and he
connects to the mobile application.
It is necessary that the blue column be
active so that others can too
also work for the app to connect!
If the logo changes color to yellow, it means that it is
the battery has weakened, and if it starts flashing red, the battery
is critically low and needs to be recharged immediately.
You can also control the state of the batteries using your mobile phone
applications. There are also on the underside of the blue device
and three openings for sound input into the interior of the device. No
cover them and do not place the device on a soft surface that
can absorb sound!
3 - 3: Charging the device battery
The device contains a lithium-ion battery that is charged with voltage
of 5V/3A, using the USB-C connector on the bottom
device. After connecting the voltage, the logo indicates
charging by emitting red, yellow or green color, u
according to the battery condition. When it is steady green,
the charging process is complete.

Selection of the operating mode and fine adjustments of the device are carried out
using the VoiceToys app. Before you start
mobile application, be sure to turn on the blue column because in
it has a bluetooth receiver with which the device
communicates with the phone. Devices connect automatically,
without any additional adjustment and react to the sound
immediately after switching on.
After you turn on the device, on the home screen of the application
(shown on page 7) select the "SpreadY" option when
color its symbol green. If the connection
successful, the blue pillar logo will blink twice, and
the mobile application will download the device settings and
display the values ​​it is currently responding to.
In the upper blue field of the application screen you can see
device name and keys for system information and help.
By pressing the gear symbol in the upper blue field
you will access the device information screen, where

it has the latest software version loaded into it.

3 - 5: Operating modes of the SpreadY device and their purpose
The SpreadY system offers three operating modes, with the following
proposed purposes:
1 - Mode: Microphone (sound response)
Purpose: Visual identification of sound propagation
2 - Mode: Memory (user set parameters)
Purpose: Memory game and learning addition
3 - Mode: Manual (user includes steps as desired)
Purpose: Analysis and synthesis of words and sentences
The mode is selected by pressing the "Mode" button, which
located in the right corner of the lower blue field of the screen
applications. In the picture on the right you can see the after screen
pressing the "Mode" button. When you touch the caption with
with the desired mode, a detailed screen will appear
setting that mode.
Access and usage methods are explained below
of each of the mentioned modes.
modes of operation of the device spread
Screen display after pressing a key
"mode"

Battery level indicators for each of the bars
Currently loaded preset and + button to create a new preset from

Sliders for adjusting the visual identification of the sound level (described in
next page)
Selection of presets, return to the main page and operating modes of the SpreadY system
Keys for selecting the display mode - analog or digital display
Device name, system information, and help
3-6: After entering the menu by pressing the "MODE" key
to select the operating mode of the device, visual identification mode
select the sound level by pressing the "Microphone" button. It will appear
is the screen shown on the previous page, in the upper part of which
in the blue box you can see the name of the device and the keys for
system information and help. Function and method of use
these keys are described on page 15.
The visual representation of sound can be analog or
digital. Keys for selecting the visual representation of sound
"Analog" and "Digital" are located in the middle of the screen
applications. In analog mode, all the pillars shine together, a
the intensity of the radiation depends on the sound level. In digital mode
the pillars always shine with maximum intensity when the sound
exceeds the level that is defined for each of them, and they light up
are one by one, depending on the sound level.
Adjust the sound level, sensitivity and decay in
analog, i.e. sound level, sensitivity and intensity
lights in the digital mode of the visual representation of sound,
is done using the sliders located in the lower half
application screen, in the "Settings" section.
The sound level is the threshold that the sound level must pass in order to
caused a light reaction. Sounds below this level are
ignored. If you need, adjust it according to
the noise level in your space so that the blue pillar does not
turns on when no one is speaking.
Sensitivity is the sensitivity of the system to a change in sound level.
If you change it to higher values, the system becomes
more sensitive, and it is possible to arouse it more easily. Moving slider
towards smaller values, a higher sound level is needed to
to start the device, that is, to turn on the lights.
Decay determines the decrease in intensity of light from blue
to the gray pillar.
Light intensity adjusts the intensity of light emission
pillar. The more you move it towards higher values,
the light pillars will shine brighter.
If you change the sound level, sensitivity,
light intensity or dimming, you can make your own settings
remember as described on the next page.
microphone mode: settings
In the left corner of the lower blue field of the application is located
"Reset" button. By pressing this key, the lower part of the screen
application looks like the image on the right.
In this layout of the lower part of the screen, the applications are located

Under the "Defaults" tab are the operating settings
which are pre-created. Each of these settings is
remembered under a specific name that represents a description
system mode when the selected setting is activated.
Under the "User" tab are the settings that you
remembered. The way to remember the settings you created is
the following:
In case you have changed any of the offered
parameters, you can remember your settings
by pressing the button located in the upper part of the screen.
A keyboard will appear on the screen. Enter a name for the creation
setting and save.
You can find your saved settings by pressing
the "Reset" button located in the left corner of the lower blue
fields of the application screen, and then to the "User" tab.
All will be displayed

now remembered.

3-7: After entering the menu by pressing the "MODE" key
to select device operating mode, memory game mode and learning mode
additions are selected by pressing the "Memory" key. It will appear
is the screen shown on the previous page, in the upper part of which

The memory game generates strings of random numbers from 1 to 5
and displays them by illuminating the columns. By setting
parameters, which is performed using the application, you can
determine the level of complexity of the game. Setting parameters
is done using the sliders located in the lower part of the screen
whose layout is shown on the previous page.
The number of series determines how many series of spark plugs will be in
during one game. The smallest number of series is 1, and the largest is 10.
The maximum size indicates how many posts we will have
use during one batch. We can set it to turn on
just one, up to all five pillars. When once
set, this parameter remains the same in all series.
The duration of the display indicates the time during which the bars will be
lit up. Time is expressed in seconds.
The duration of the break indicates the time during which the bars will be
extinguished between the two series. Time is expressed in
seconds.
The intensity of the light determines the strength of the light that the pillars will emit
be illuminated.
If you change the sound level, sensitivity,
light intensity or dimming, you can make your own settings
remember as described on page 19.
Below the slider for setting game parameters are
PLAY, REPEAT and ARROW key. Their purpose
is explained on the next page.
In the lower blue field of the application screen is the "Reset" button,
the use of which is described on page 19. You can always go back
to the home screen of the "VoiceToys" application by pressing the key
with a picture of a house, which is in the middle of the lower blue
application screen fields.
memory mode: settings
You start the game by pressing the key. The stairs will start to
are turned on, and the screen will show the scheme according to which they are
lit. The layout of this screen is shown in the image to the right.
The numbers in the left column indicate the ignition series number
pillar. In this example the game was limited to 5 series.
The numbers in the right column indicate how many columns there were
lit in that series. In this example, there were in the first series
lit 3 pillars, in the second also 3, in the third 2, and in
fourth and fifth one each.
The numbers in the bottom row indicate how many columns are specified
colors were lit in total during the game. In this one
for example, there were 2 blue ones, 3 green ones, 1 yellow one, and 1 red one
gray by 2. The number 10 in the right corner indicates how many there are
total number of posts lit during the game.
Colored fields indicate bars of a certain color that are
were lit during each of the series. In this example in the first
green, yellow and gray columns were lit in one series, blue in the other,
red and gray, and so on.
The application randomly selects which columns will be placed
to light in which series, and how many columns will be lit in which series in
relative to the given maximum display size that
set with a parameter
described on the previous one
foreign.

you start a new game with
the same parameters that are
previously assigned.

game, with the same default
parameters.

same key.

Determining the way of activation and display of lit pillars

next section.
One indicates the option in which it will turn on and stay on
only one column. The pillar can be turned on by pressing
field that is bordered with the color of the column we want

We can light the pillars in order from blue to gray and back

Any indicates an option in which pressing the field that is
bordered by the color of the pillar we want to light, we light it
one or more pillars. Turn off the column by pressing again
colored field.
Random indicates the option where the application will randomize
light one pillar with the pattern. The pillar will light up after
press the PLAY button, and we will turn it off and be ready for
subsequent ignition by pressing the button. Colored field
indicates which pillar is lit.
You can always return to the "VoiceToys" home screen
applications by pressing the button with the image of a house, which is located there
in the middle of the bottom blue field of the application screen.

4 -1: System description and safety notes
JumpY is a multipurpose light panel measuring 120x30
cm, primarily intended for audio-visual and sensor-
motor stimulation. It can react to the sound level
using a built-in microphone of exceptional sensitivity, and can
to connect wirelessly with the corresponding sensor
distance. Using the mobile application, his are chosen
numerous operating modes.
The light panel is not intended for any physical contact
with the user! Do not touch or hit his
light surface!
4 - 2: Commissioning the system
The JumpY panel is intended for wall mounting. Instructions for
assembly is on the next page. If necessary
to move it often, at your own risk
you can also use without fastening. The JumpY panel is
the only VoiceToys device that must be connected to
electrical network during operation. Therefore, before using it
make sure you have a power source nearby. The device is powered up
cable with a USB-C connector. The connection is located on the bottom
right side of the light panel, just below
switch.
Use it to connect a standard USB
adapter, minimum 5V/3A to electricity
network. Turn on the switch. The device is
initializes by emitting red. After
short period of device initialization
it starts working, and in that mode in which
worked during previous use.
Detailed information about the device
Switch
USB-C port for power
ASSEMBLY of the device
The total dimensions of the device are 1200x300 mm.
The device is fastened using three screws with a head diameter of 8mm and a body of 5mm, length
minimum 100 mm.
According to the attached diagram, drill holes on the wall and put dowels in them
attach screws or hooks. After that, carefully place the device on them.
Leave enough space on the lower right side for the power connection and
switch control.
After assembly, attach the supplied self-adhesive brackets to the corners
device.

which will broadcast reactions for

4 - 4: JumpY operating modes and their purpose
Light panel with distance sensor JumpY offers
several operating modes, with the following suggested uses:
1 - Mode: Reaction
1a - Microphone (reaction to sound)
Purpose: Visual identification of sound level
1b - Distance sensor (reaction to distance)
Purpose: Visual identification of distance
2 - Mode: Snoezelen (user sets parameters)
Purpose: Soothing multicolored light
3 - Mode: Tap Game
Purpose: Exercise of patience, focus, gross motor skills
4 - Mode: Differences
Purpose: Color recognition exercise, patience, focus
5 - Mode: Rhythmic pattern
Purpose: Recognition and repetition of rhythmic patterns
6 - Mode: Phonetics
Purpose: Practicing formants and sibilants

MODE: reaction
4-4-1: After pressing the "MODE" key you have entered the
menu for selecting the operating mode of the device, visual mode
identify the sound level and distance you select by pressing
"Reaction" button. The screen shown on page 27 will appear

how to use these keys are described on page 26.
In the middle of the application screen, there are identifying images
the current operating mode of the device (left) and the light display mode
panel (right image). You can see the appearance of this part of the screen
in the picture below.
The left image on the screen shows the operating mode of the device which is
marked with the word "Microphone" and the corresponding symbol, all
while the distance sensor is not on. With these settings
the light panel reacts to the sound. When the distance sensor
turn on, the light panel stops responding to the sound and reacts
only on changing the distance between the sensor and the object, and on
on the screen of the application is written "Distance" and corresponding
symbol. When the distance sensor turns off the app and everything
its functions, as well as the light panel, automatically return to
"Microphone" mode, i.e. they respond to sound again.
The right image located in the middle of the application screen
shows the current appearance of the light panel. When you tap
picture, a window opens (picture below) in which you can change
appearance of the light panel according to the offered options, on
the way described on the next page.
MODE: reaction
Adjusting the sound level, sensitivity and visual inertia
sound representation is done using sliders located in
in the lower half of the application screen, in the "Source" section.
The sound level is the threshold that the sound level must pass in order to
caused a light reaction. Sounds below this level are
ignored. If necessary, adjust it according to the level
noise in your space so that the light panel does not
turns on when no one is speaking.
Sensitivity is the sensitivity of the system to a change in sound level.
If you change it to higher values, the system becomes
more sensitive, and it is possible to arouse it more easily. Moving slider
towards smaller values, a higher sound level is needed to
to start the device, that is, to turn on the lights.
Inertia represents the speed of the light panel's reaction to
sound excitation.
When you turn on the distance sensor, in the "Source" section,
The Range slider appears. You can adjust the sensitivity with it
of the light display on changing the distance of the obstacle from
sensor distance. The lower the value on the slider, the brighter
on the panel will respond faster and fill the screen more.
Setting the display of lights on the light panel is done
using the sliders located in the lower half of the screen
applications, in the "View" section, and slider functions in different
screen display variants are:
"Rainbow" - you can adjust the Falling slider
the decay rate of light response to sound.
"Fields" - the Fields slider appears next to the Decline slider.
With it, you determine how many multi-colored fields there will be
split view of the lights on the light panel.
"Top" - Decline and two more sliders appear. Decline
of the peak determines at what speed the peak will decline, ie
the red line showing the maximum volume reached
sound. The peak hold determines how long the peak will last
stand still before it starts to fall down the light
panel.
"Window" - Sliders Drop Top and Keep Top
have the same function as in the previous case, and the term "top"
refers to the white line that shows the sound level.
If you change the sound level, sensitivity,
light intensity or dimming, you can make your own settings
remember as described on page 19.
mode: reaction - distance sensor
After you turn on the distance sensor it will automatically
connect to the light panel, and the application screen will
look like the picture on the right. Detailed setting of his
function is described on the previous page.
Turn on the distance sensor after placing it on
the maximum distance from the object from which you want to measure
distance. At the moment of switching on, the sensor measures the initial value
distance, and any distance changes are displayed in the ratio
on her.
Place it so that the side of the sensor they are on
round openings should be directed towards the object from which it is measured
distance. For example: if the distance sensor
you place two round openings under the trampoline
found on it should be directed upwards, as
is shown in the picture.
You can see the charge status on the app screen
of the battery located in the distance sensor. Don't
wait for the battery to be completely discharged, instead of charging it
by connecting the USB-C cable to the designated place
which is marked with an arrow in the picture below.

the set includes 5 devices of different colors.
5 - 1: System description and safety notes
SpaceY is a system consisting of 5 smart speakers
primarily intended for practicing identification and
spatial localization of sound in people with disabilities
by hearing. They connect wirelessly and are powered by
built-in rechargeable batteries. They are controlled exclusively
VoiceToys mobile app. They broadcast easily
recognizable sounds sorted into groups of five
sounds.
5 - 2: Commissioning
The speakers are turned on by setting the switch located there
from the bottom of the device to position 1. Lights on the speakers
they start emitting light in the color of the device. It is necessary
for the blue speaker to be turned on so that the others can too
they work! After turning on all speakers, start
mobile application. Na početnom ekranu aplikacije kliknite
to the image of the speakers when the background behind them turns green
color. If the speakers are connected correctly, the lights on
njima će se ugasiti. This means they are ready to go.
If during operation the lights start to glow orange, that
means that the battery is weak, and if they start flashing
red, the battery is critically low and needs it
odmah napuniti. You can also control the state of the batteries
using a mobile application.
DO NOT grab the speakers by the soundboard - white
board made of base and EVA foam with the mark ! Postoji opasnost
to fall away. Please hold the speaker only by the
plastic case!
Detailed information about the device
SpaceY , the front of the device
Indicator
light
Resonant
plate
5 - 3: Assembly and placement in space
Place the speakers in the room so that they are in front, left,
to the right, behind, and one above the listener or the group he is with
works. In the case of wall mounting, it is necessary to drill holes in
wall and place screws with a diameter of 6 millimeters in them, with
angled head. Attach the speakers to the screws using
hole located on the back of the speaker.
5 - 4: Charging the device battery
The device contains a lithium-ion battery that is charged with voltage
of 5V/3A, using the USB-C connector on the back
device. After connecting the voltage, the light indicates
charging by emitting red, yellow or green color, u
according to the battery condition. When it is steady green,
the charging process is complete. When you stop working with
SpaceY speakers, the lights on them will turn on like
a reminder to turn them off. Do not leave them on without
needs, in order to avoid unnecessary consumption of the battery.

5 - 5: Mobile application features
After you turn on the device, on the home screen of the application
(shown on page 7) select the "SpaceY" option when
color its symbol green. If the connection
successfully, the lights on the speakers will turn off and they are ready
for work.
In the upper green field of the application screen you can see
device name and keys for system information and help.
The functions of these keys are explained on page 16. Immediately
below them there is a section where you can see the balance
battery charge for each speaker.
In the upper part of the screen there are the following buttons:
- in the upper left corner, there is an indication of what time it is
elapsed from the moment of activation of a certain sound to
giving answers. After each confirmed correct
answer, you will see the time for which the correct answer was given.
Time is reactivated with the activation of the next sound;
- in the upper right corner there is an indication of the average
time to answer all tasks assigned by
the start of the application
- there is a help button in the lower left corner.
Pressing this button will turn on the light for a short time
to the speaker from which the sound comes. The emergency light will
will be active until you turn it off by pressing again
button.
- in the lower right corner there is a reset button
both time indicators.
In the lower part of the screen there are:
- slider for volume regulation and indication
speakers produce. The initial value is 25, it is possible
boost up to 30. Volume up or down affects everything
speakers alike.
- five fields with pictures of animals, framed by colors that
correspond to the speakers used to emit sound.
When the speakers are on, only the fields are displayed
active speakers. By clicking on the image in the field of a certain color,
the corresponding sound is heard from the speaker in the color of the field that is
pressed. If necessary, you can broadcast the same again
sound without affecting the timing.
mobile application features
- if you want to change the arrangement of sounds, press
one of the arrows or located above the blue,
that is, the yellow field. The pictures will change places, and so will the speakers
react according to the new schedule.
- there are fields in the positions below the blue and yellow fields
green and red colors used for evaluation
answers. A correct answer is scored by pressing green,
and incorrect on the red field. It will be heard on that occasion
characteristic sounds of approval along with colorful
lights emitted by the speaker from which the given sound came
for correct, or sound of disapproval along with red
light emitted by all speakers for incorrect answers. At
keys will show numbers indicating the number of data
correct and incorrect answers.
In the lower green field of the screen there is a "Mode" button.
Pressing this key will bring up groups of sounds that
you can choose (animals, vehicles, household appliances,
instruments and nature). By choosing one of the groups of sounds
the images at the bottom of the screen will change and appear
images from the group of sounds you selected. By clicking on the pictures
new sounds corresponding to the images will be emitted.

6 - Automatic device update
in two simple steps
YOUR MOBILE DEVICE
DOWNLOADING UPDATE
FROM THE VOICETOYS CLOUD,
VIA INTERNET CONNECTION
YOUR MOBILE DEVICE
CONNECTS TO WIFI NETWORK
YOUR VOICETOYS DEVICE
AND AUTOMATICALLY UPDATES IT
C L O U D
The illustration shows VibeY. The same goes for any other VoiceToys device.
6 -1 : What is device update?
VoiceToys are digitally controlled devices - they contain
microcontroller that has its own program - firmware.
Updating the firmware allows us to them
we improve with new functions and correct any ones
defects in the old. Therefore, this user manual
will be updated and changed. When a new version is available
firmware, the app will notify you and offer an update.
If the update is optional, you can postpone it until later
by pressing the "cancel" button. If it is necessary
update, you must perform it before continuing.
6 - 2 : Update flow
1 - Start and download updates - After pressing the
Update button, the new firmware is downloaded from the Internet
memory of your mobile device. For this step
it is necessary that the device is connected to the Internet.
dfdfgdg
After the update is downloaded, your VoiceToys device enters
into Update mode, that is, it becomes a Wi-Fi access point.
The device stops working normally and you can see on it
blue lights.
2 - Connecting to WiFi VoiceToys device - Android
mobile devices will automatically take you to WiFi
your phone's settings, while on iPhones it
you have to do it manually by running Settings
of your device and select the WiFi option. If WiFi on
your phone is not turned on, turn it on.
Detailed explanation
SETTINGS WIFI
In the list of WiFi networks, find the VoiceToys update network of yours
device and connect to it:
After you have connected to the VoiceToys update network,
return to the VoiceToys app, where it will welcome you
the following notice:
e
From this moment you just have to wait
the update is sent from the phone to the device. Don't turn it off
until it restarts automatically. After that, the process
is finished!
Detailed explanation
6 -1 : Guarantee
VoiceToys D.O.O. as the author and producer of the VoiceToys system
gives every user a full 24-hour guarantee
months after purchasing the product.
If it appears within 24 months from the date of sale
any technical irregularity, the device will be free of charge and in
be replaced by a new one as soon as possible.
After the expiration of the warranty period, or in case of physical
damage or consequences of handling contrary to this instruction,
we will fix any possible malfunction on your devices with
minimum possible compensation for service and spare parts.
6 - 2 : Maintenance
VoiceToys devices do not require anything special
maintenance, except cleaning with a soft cloth and mild
disinfectants.
6 - 3 : Service
The device is serviced exclusively by the manufacturer - VoiceToys D.O.O.
Please contact us directly in case of problems
us and that you do not attempt to repair the devices yourself.
Except for the "JumpYY" device, they all have lithium batteries that
they lose their capacity over time. If you notice significant
decrease in battery life, request a replacement
our service. The battery is not intended to be user-replaceable.
